_meta.purpose: Extension B hand-curated Open Dental mapping. Consumed by praxis_deid/extractors/b_*.py to produce claims_raw.csv at de-id time. Derived from expected_mapping_open_dental.json + expert audit. DO NOT regenerate from the wizard — the wizard's output is 78% reliable per the Wizard-1 validation gate (commit 6b19ae9). Hand-edits to this file MUST cite the Open Dental manual page they are based on.

payer_category.notes: Open Dental stores carrier name as free text. Mapping carrier name to canonical payer_category enum requires a per-practice lookup table or transformation rules. Operator must supply the carrier-name -> category mapping.

payment_date.source_expression: (SELECT MAX(claimproc.DateCP) FROM claimproc WHERE claimproc.ClaimNum = claim.ClaimNum AND claimproc.Status = 1)
payment_date.notes: Latest claimproc.DateCP for Status=1 (Received/paid). NULL if no procs paid. Per Open Dental docs, Status=1 is the canonical 'Received' code; Status=2 is Preauth (pre-authorization, not a payment) and is a common source of analyst error.

denial_date.notes: Open Dental does not flag claims as 'denied' explicitly. Denials show up as ResponseStatus in clearinghouseslog. Practices vary in how they record outright denials vs. partial pays.

authorization_required.notes: Inferred from presence of pre-auth string. Practices that don't track pre-auth in this field will return false even when auth was used.

authorization_date.notes: No explicit auth-date column in Open Dental claim table.

denial_reason_category.notes: Denial reasons live as free text in clearinghouseslog.ResponseDetail. Mapping to canonical category requires NLP or a curated lookup.

status.source_expression: CASE WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'R' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'A' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'S' THEN 'submitted' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'I' THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'H' THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus IN ('U','W') THEN 'pending' ELSE 'pending' END
status.notes: ClaimStatus is a 1-char enum: U=Unsent, W=Waiting, P=Probable, S=Sent, R=Received, H=Hold, I=Sent-verified, A=Adjustment. Open Dental does not differentiate paid-in-full from partial in this field — 'partial' must be derived from amount comparison.

pre_verified.source_expression: EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM insverify WHERE insverify.FKey = claim.PatNum AND insverify.VerifyType = 0 AND insverify.DateLastVerified >= DATE_SUB(claim.DateService, INTERVAL 90 DAY))
pre_verified.notes: Pre-verification heuristic: any insverify row for the patient within 90 days before service. The 90-day window is a guess; practices' verification refresh intervals vary.

transformations.status: CASE WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'R' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus IN ('U','W') THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'S' THEN 'submitted' ELSE 'pending' END
transformations.payer_category: /* practice-specific carrier-name -> canonical-payer-category lookup table required */

notes: Carrier-name to payer-category requires a practice-specific lookup. Many practices have only a few carriers; suggest the wizard prompt the operator for a mapping table.
Pre-verification window is a guess; should be a configurable parameter.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: payment_date corrected from claimproc.Status=2 to claimproc.Status=1 (the canonical Received code per Open Dental docs). Status=2 is Preauth.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: status CASE expanded to cover H/I/A enum values.
